title: Ear-EEG Sleep Monitoring 2023 (EESM23)
description: The Ear-EEG Sleep Monitoring 2023 (EESM23) dataset comprises nightly electroencephalography recordings from 10 healthy participants collected during 2020-2022. The dataset includes polysomnography and ear-EEG measurements from initial recordings, with subsequent recordings consisting of ear-EEG data alone. Each recording session contains both a video task sequence and a subsequent sleep monitoring sequence, with all recordings conducted in participants' home environments.
methods_description: EEG data were collected using ear-EEG equipment in EEGLAB .set format. The first two recordings included polysomnography (PSG) measurements alongside ear-EEG. For initial recordings, equipment was mounted by researchers in the afternoon; for subsequent recordings, participants self-mounted equipment prior to bedtime. Participants performed video tasks followed by overnight sleep monitoring, with manual event marking via trigger buttons to distinguish task and sleep sequences.
